text: I refer a friend to get $50. But when I claimed the $50 they refused to give it to me. Another incident, I went to a T-Mobile store to make a payment. That young lady, one of the staff took a picture of my account with her personal cell phone. I asked her why she said because this computer is not working and I wanted to go to the other computer. I asked why did you take a picture of my account using your personal cell phone. She answered me in a non professional way. I put in a complaint with T-Mobile but they never got back to me and the staff is still working in the same location.

Bought an LG Stylo 6...it is JUNK...I cant't earn a living because the phone does not work.
A Tech support MANAGER told me, "You are the one who chose to buy that phone." (Even though T-Mobile has replaced the phone once and is now "offering" to replace it again...with the same piece of junk model...!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!)
NO KIDDING, he said that.
DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH THESE CLOWNS!!!!
Additionally EVERY T-Mobile store attendant I have ever spoken to in 3 different retail locations (Tyrone Mall, 4th Street No and 66th St N in St Petersburg, FL  has been rude, condescending and unprofessional (sorry if that's redundant.
This experience has cost me more than the price of a Samsung Galaxy S21 Ultra 5G in lost earnings.
I drive (er drove) for Uber and Lyft up until a few hours ago...this has been going on for OVER A MONTH (!) and T-Mobile has done nothing to help me.
FYI
LG Electronics no longer makes cell phones..."After years of selling phones with software that is not compatible with their own hardware", according to one T-Mobile rep at the Tyrone Mall location....
Meaning that T-Mobile willingly and with full knowledge took my money for merchandise they knew to be intrinsically defective.

I went in to get my phone wiped as I got a new phone because my screen cracked. I think his name Abdullah - the guy who helped me. He was very knowledgeable, competent and friendly. He helped me out big time.  I so appreciate it. Thank you.

edits.text: I went to the T Mobile store on Stacey Rd in Allen TX (June 9 or 10 2021); I was very happy with the friendliness and professionalism of the employees who assisted me.  We signed up for T Mobile internet which I am excited to see how it goes.  I felt very much taken care of at this store.  Thank you.

text: The truth about T-mobile.  0lans and rates are very good. However coverage is very poor. Don't get swayed by there advertising. When T-mobile and sprint merged coverage got worst.  We were told that they were changing out the cell phone towers and things would improve.  Nope never did. Worse ever. Stay with Verizon better coverage and good rates. We are changing.

Research this company thoroughly before accepting offers that sound good. They're disorganized and their customer service reps on the phone will flat out lie to you. You'll also hear different things every time you call or visit a store. I finished an 18 month phone lease and wanted to upgrade my plan and phone. My account had a $0 balance. AT&T offered a better deal, so I called T-Mobile/Sprint to discuss my options. They transferred me to what they called their "Retention Dept." The rep presented numerous benefits of staying with TM/Sprint, such as an educator's discount, so I went into the store to sign up for those account features. Unfortunately, the store reps said that what the Retention Rep offered was, "impossible," because some of the things didn't make sense. For example, the educator's discount hadn't even existed in well over a year. I called customer service again and, after waiting on hold for 52 minutes, was again directed to the Retention Dept. This time, the rep said she'd give me a lower rate. I asked if she could note on my account what we agreed to and she said she shouldn't need to because the changes had already been made and will begin at the next billing cycle. Since the new plan was settled, I went back into the store to upgrade my phone. At that point, the store reps said there weren't any changes on my account. I again called customer service and asked to be transferred to their Retention Dept, as the in store reps suggested. The rep on the phone said the Dept. doesn't even exist, but he tried assisting me anyway. He also promised things that I later found out weren't entirely true regarding phone replacement, but said he noted things on my account. When I visited the store again he next day, I was told that his notes were vague and didn't include some of the things I came in to discuss. I again called customer service, but this time had the phone rep talk with the store employee. Finally, we were able to work out an acceptable phone plan. Keep in mind, while all this was happening I was also trying to upgrade to a better phone. I had finished my 18 month lease and was told I had a zero balance. However, they wouldn't allow me to upgrade to another phone unless I paid $200+ or sent my phone back. I was fine with sending the phone back, but was then told I couldn't because it has a small crack in the screen. If they were going to re-lease the phone to another customer, I'd understand not accepting it back. However, the model is 3 or so years old, so I know they'll just use it for scrap or toss it entirely anyway. I offered paying some to have the small crack repaired, but they rejected the idea. On another note, I was never told that I would still owe anything on the phone once the lease ended. That doesn't make any sense. I was then told by a store rep that I could bring in any other phone that would turn on and that didn't have any damage to trade in for an upgraded phone model. However, when I brought in a phone like they asked, I was told that the phone options they suggested I upgrade to were unavailable at that store. I eventually ended up visiting four stores in the area and they all seemed to say different things. The last store I visited yesterday had the phone that a previous rep suggested I upgrade to, but the in-store reps said that sending the phone back, even with a potential repair, would still not entirely bring my phone balance to zero. (Remember, my 18 month lease had been completed and paid in full.) Even the store employees there said that the Sprint/T-Mobile merger has been a disaster and that they can't wait for things to settle down because they've seen many customers come in and complain about the same problems I'm facing. People were talked into leases that were misleading. If you look online, you'll see that others had the same problem. This is not a problem that's uniquely mine. To summarize, the customer service reps on the phone are not on the same page with each other and will flat-out lie about plan options to keep you with the company. The customer service reps in the store are also not on the same page as the ones on the phone. However, the in-store reps were at least honest about things. I've never see such a dishonest, disorganized company. I've wasted roughly six hours on the phone with them and probably four more in various store locations the past several days. I cannot wait to leave this company and STRONGLY recommend you look elsewhere for service.

Veryyyyyy bad company bad service i had very bad experience i dont recommend any one to go to this business my friend ask me i saved him not to go there i recommend him Metro i love metro and my friend love metro as well they charged me $113.39  more there representative told me your billing cycle is due on 14 i changed my line to metro on 13 but they still charged me more my account number was this 973162929 thats the account number i paid the balance today and veryyyyyy bad company never to there the account was under my name and there collection team send me the bill for $238.43 the comapany is convergent. 
Lier i spoke to their representative more then 20 they all say something different.

I bought a phone they told me they would comp me 600$ for my phone they took 25$ off my first 2months of my bill  then charged me 25$ more for equipment charges??? So the phone I traded in I got nothing for. Not to mention the service sucked

PLEASE READ THIS BEFORE CONSIDERING SIGNING UP WITH T MOBILE. I would have given 0 stars if it allowed me to. I do not recommend using this provider. They falsely advertise their promotions. When signing up we were told by multiple customer service representatives that T-Mobile would cover 100% of the remaining balances of the phone lines with our previous provider. After being with T-Mobile for 3 months the balance has not been paid. I spent multiple days on phone with multiple customer service representatives. Each representative was not helpful. I was told more than 3 times that I would be sent prepaid credit cards for $600 on each card. I never received a card. I was told multiple times that a supervisor would contact me on that day. I never received a call. I have not gotten reimbursed for the payment I made on the remaining balance of my phone lines with my previous provider.

Switch to T-Mobile a few months ago from Verizon to save a few pennies and I thought get better service. What a joke. I regret switching to T-Mobile every single day. They are the absolute worst with the worst coverage imaginable. Constantly catching their reps in lies about service area and 5G coverage. Several of their higher level reps have admitted to me T Mobile coverage is severely lacking and they have lots of problems with their own service. T Mobile has tried double and triple charging me, changing my plans in my pricing without my permission, and I’m having to call them literally every month to make sure they don’t screw me over on my phone bill. Who knows how many people don’t catch him and are getting totally reamed by T Mobile as they maliciously adjust billing, pricing and plans for their customers. I hope Santa takes a big fat steaming shat in every one of their stockings.
It's a boga company to stay away from her as far as possible.
I scheduled a call back & their option to press #1 to reach a rep doesn't work. The wait time is an hour. If something tells you to press 1 then use a different approach.

Asked me to call the customer service to get it sorted. Been on the line for 30-min waiting for a representative and nobody answers. I made an inquiry through the T-Mobile web site chat to learn more on the Magenta 55+plan, and if switching to T-Mobile from my current provider would include a free iPhone.

Never got a straight response!
They asked what kind of phone we had (iPhone X), was it good shape (yes). They responded, "I can call you and switch you now". 
I replied you have not answered my question, if we switch from our current carrier to the Magenta 55+plan are we eligible for the free iPhone with the iPhone X trade in?  " Yes, I did read my text!".  

One more time yes or no if we switch from our current carrier to the Magenta 55+plan are we eligible for the free iPhone with the iPhone X trade in? "I answered you already" 

At which point I responded never mind you either do not understand or your being obnoxious.   

If it is this hard to spend money, I can only image how hard it would be if there is a problem.  Spent >13 minutes in the Chat and still do not have a straight answer; which suggest the answer is no! But did not want to say.
They refuse to give me a loaner phone after my phone was backordered. So 3 weeks without a phone. And their solution is for me to buy another phone in the meantime.

I would like to personally thank Nicholas Kotschwar, from the 14899 W 151st St T-Mobile Store. 

Nicholas was very knowledgeable, not to mention extremely helpful with resolving the issues I was experiencing with my old Sprint phone (ie old Sprint phone firmware issues / would not boot) and was able to assist me with my phone swap (from Sprint to T-Mobile) which I was unable to myself online. It was Nicholas' quick actions that resolved my issue and had this happy customer on his way with a new (working) phone.

Thank you Nicholas!

If you want to experience the worst in customer care, then T-Mobile is for you.  If you want to experience the worst in 5G service, then T-Mobile is the way to go.  If you want to do business with thieves, then T-Mobile is the service for you.  I am so tired of calling the support center for the same issues all the time and being lied to.  And then the T-Mobile phone puppets want you to assure them that they took care of all your concerns - NOT!!!  This is the most unreliable phone and customer service I have ever experienced.  I wish I read these reviews prior to signing up for T-Mobile.

I love T-Mobile glad to be back with T-Mobile I have sprint sprint was terrible I was with sprint for seven years and I had to leave and get right back with T-Mobile the prices are way better my bill is not high Sprint will always ask me for money every week Sprint is so bad and plus they’re going out of business anyway so I recommend everybody come over to T-Mobile I would never ever go back over to Sprint even if they did go back into their own company I would never ever go back to spring again they treated me so terrible sprint gave me hell for a 7 to 8 years T-Mobile is the best

Update:  I'm still with spectrum and the service is very inexpensive and great. I still use the same phone and have no problems. Very glad I left t-mobile. I'm shocked t-mobile is still in business. Simply the best? Maybe the best at sucking.

edits.text: They suck. They were nice as could be to rope me in now they cant be bothered. Internet was so slow if I got it at all, I had to use wifi to get anything. Boyfriend on another line would get his messages days later. I brought my phone in and they said everything looked ok, so it was probably the tower being switched to 5g. I waited  a couple of weeks still no internet without spectrum wifi. Bring phone back in they switched out the chip, and said that's all they could do,  Having problems with galaxy phones and towers. Told to call customer service to fix problem. Spent an hour trying to fix with customer service and finally gave up trying. Went to just buy a prepaid because I was sick of wasting time with them. I decided to try spectrum because I really like my phone and its paid for.  Asked t mobile to help me back up my boyfriends data because I needed to leave t mobile as I needed internet and I was told "they can help you with that" meaning spectrum. Sooo glad I didnt finance a phone with them. They were pushing for it and I'm so glad I didnt get stuck with them. When I was switching from att they were talking about how bad att was and I agree I was paying way to much but at least I got internet. T mobile was 70 a month for slow to no internet, calls not going through and messages days after they were left. I hope spectrum is my last stop with the cell phone issues because I've wasted way to much time on this.

text: All I ever get is no network connection 98% of the time no matter where I go, I keep calling costumer service and they send me through the same process which never works and takes up about 30min+. Terrible technical support

Not one person is native English speaking for support.

Back when 2g was a thing, T-Mobile disabled one of the towers in town and were converting it 3g (at least this is what they told me). After that, I had no signal for 90% of my day while living in a major city, where I previously had signal. I called and complained. They sent me some equipment to get better signal in the house. I now had no signal for only 80% of the day! Wow. I called repeatedly and they wouldn't do anything about it, which is breaching the contract by not providing service.  So I shut off my account and sent the equipment back through a sanctioned method. A month later, I got a bill for the rest of the conteact AND the equipment I returned and they refused to work with me. It's around $1600. They continue to post it to my credit too as I will NEVER pay them for this unlawful bill. I hired a lawyer and made one of their third party collectors drop it, which sent it back to TMOBILE, and they sent it to a new collector. So, plan 2, we're going to continue to fight it and tell everyone about T-Mobile's embarrassingly horrible service.

My Brother-in Law passed away on April 24th after a long painful battle with Lung Cancer.  I called T-Mobile to cancell his service which went well at first.  They offered their condolences and cancelled the service.  A few days later, they took the next month auto pay out of his checking account which had no money except his disability in it which would be coming back out after the death certificate made it to social security.  Now that left a negative because of t-mobile.  I called them and they told me to just use his phone for another month because they could not reverse that charge.  My brother in law had added my name to his checking account the month before so I could close it out and stop all auto pays.  Well, since we have the same bank, his negative balance was taken out of my checking account.  Thank you T-Mobile.  I am so glad I am with AT&T.  They would never have treated a family member of someone who passed away like that.  I give you no stars.
So tired of t mobile. I've been a loyal customer with sprint every month is paid on time and I still have a down payment that hasn't been updated like it should've been. Do not go through tmobile

text: TMobile, The Worse Cell phone carrier ever. The only thing you need to know is that you should go back to your original phone service. I'm going back to Verizon. I didn't know the definition of bad phone service until I joined T-Mobile.

While T Mobile has easy to understand pricing and great deals for your calls, texts, and data — they also have terrible coverage and spotty (at best) service. In Galveston TX on vacation, could not get more than one bar inside our rental house unless we went to the second story, then got 2 bars. It says LTE, but you can’t open Google maps or look for nearby restaurants unless you can find a coffee shop or bar with free Wifi.

In Layton, UT, where I live, I can’t get service in the basement of my house about 1/2 the time. In my grocery store, trying to talk to my husband, call got dropped 3 times. Talking to my sister in Virginia while walking around my neighborhood, call got dropped twice. Went to my doctor’s office in SLC and couldn’t get coverage in the biggest shopping mall in downtown.

I used to have Verizon and switched my whole family because they were more expensive. After 4 months with T Mobile, I wish I could go back. I’m moving out of the country in two months, so at this point it wouldn’t be worth it to go through all the trouble.

T Mobile is a waste of my money and my time.

Been a nightmare. Poor customer services. Customer service seems to read from a script...which is fine until a question that isn't on the script related to service then they get all kinds of confused. They tell ya what you want  to hear but don't deliver. Happy to take your money though. Expected a higher level of professionalism, just wasn't there.

So difficult to write it all. I cannot. Essentially, walked in to switch from poor customer support and traded for garbage service. Was never told both towers that service our area were under construction and we cannot get cell service at all. Cannot listen to music from Spotify or other for my 2yr daughter on the way to school; let alone make a phone call. Service rep I complained to days after switching never bothered to call back. The worst decision I've ever made with cell service. I'd rather have no phone at all after this treatment. More to the story, but why bother.

I have been with sprint for about 6 years now and have never had any issues until T-Mobile took over. I ordered a new phone and paid for it and never received a confirmation for my order, or a notification that they shipped the phone but they took the money out of my account. When I would try to look up my order, it would say it didn't exist. So after two days of not hearing anything from them about my phone and seeing the money come out of my account, I got ahold of them. They assured me my phone would be here that day via UPS and that I would also be receiving a T-Mobile SIM to replace my Sprint SIM. After I got off the phone, I received a notification that said FedEx would deliver my package two days from that day. I was a little annoyed but was just happy to finally receive a notification that I even purchased the phone and that I would be receiving it. The phone ended up coming that day via FedEx and I thought that was the end of it. Nope. I tried for an entire day to activate the phone and couldn't do it. I was even on the phone with the company and they couldn't do it either. I got disconnected with the operator and decided to switch back to my sprint SIM to see if that would help. As soon as I switched the SIM cards and had my original Sprint SIM in, the phone activated and started to work. I am normally a very understanding person but I will keep the Sprint SIM in for as long as I can and when they force me to switch it to T-Mobile, I will change carriers unfortunately.

My name is Ronald I was helped by T-Mobile customer representative Abigail! She was great she helped me with all of my needs concerns and was very business professional I appreciate the business I have at T-Mobile thank you so very much I'm proud of you T-Mobile customer! Abigail went above and beyond expectations thank you for being the group customer representative Abigail!

edits.text: Today I was helped by T-Mobile representative Pia. She was from the Philippines. She was kind, polite, exceeded expectations, and was very business professional!!! She really made me feel like a great T-Mobile customer!!! He went above and beyond her job!!! We need more customer service Representatives like her!!!

text: TMobile will Rob you!! There’s always something new with the charges!! They charge you $20, for each line, if it’s late. 3 phones $60!!! Really??!! I have 4 lines with Metro, that has great service, for $90 a month!! And no late charges. Will pay off the other 2 phones and leave this God forbidden company!!

I was on Sprint for a while after they were bought by T-Mobile and it was doing fine. We went into a store looking for a trade-in but we left with nothing but T-Mobile service. Allegedly they use the same network, but this is plainly a lie as T-Mobile is simply the worst cell phone company out there. On the Sprint connection that technically should be under T-Mobile, everything was speedy and connection was very consistent. Once switching to T-Mobile, the only thing consistent was how many times the signal would abruptly cut for no reason. It is absolutely ridiculous. For the first time in my entire life I made a call and the cell service dropped it. They just cut the call and the service was totally unusable for like five minutes. This has now happened several times and it is totally unacceptable. I went back into the store to see if there was anything with my phone that was the problem, as there is no logical explanation to how bad this company is, and they had nothing to say except "sorry". This is indicative to the business model that T-Mobile has. Their commercials are actually really funny now that i am using their service as it almost seems like parody to what they provide. Everything they say runs so counter to the actual user experience that i can't help but laugh at them. They promise the world and then your calls get dropped over again and all they can say is "sorry". Don't waste your money on this awful service. Every other major phone company is better than this garbage.

I am with T Mobile for last 6 years and last week wanted to switch to Verizon as Verizon had very  good deal on iphones, buy one iphone 12 or iphone 12 pro and get another one free. On top of it, you get $450 credit per line if you don't need anew phone. I called T Mobile to make a switch and the customer service rep ( seems like he was from a call enter in Philippines, requested me to hold off and promised me that someone from T Mobile will get back to me within 24 tp 48 hours. Which turned out to be a LIE as no one called me. I called T mobile after 48 hours and the customer service rep who answered my call said we don’t have any deal to match with Verizon. Later I received survey from T Mobile and I honestly provided answers to reflect how I was cheated and how T Mobile stopped me from switching to Verizon. After that, a customer service supervisor called me on my cell phone and left me a voice mail message suggesting they can match Verizon deal and asked me to call him. I called him on Thur, June 24 and a rep answered my call. I asked him to connect me to the supervisor who left me a message he said let me check if he is in the office. He was also from call center in Philippines and he said he could not locate him. This is the issue with call centers located in other part of the world and you don't know if a person is telling you the truth or lying. He said they have many different buildings and it is not possible for him to track him down. The bottom line...you never trust T Mobile  reps, a different rep will tell you something completely different and they don't care for their customers or honor their words. This is simply unethical business practice to cheat customers. STAY AWAY from T Mobile.

I can barely give one star to the lack of customer service that I have gotten from Sprint/t-mobile. After 12 years of being with the company I have recently switched and I’m still trying to get my account resolved. I sat for two hours with customer service reps who have absolutely no authority or ability to work with customers. They say the same standard lines repeatedly with absolutely no care or concern for the customer. If I could delete the 12 years I worked with the company and all the money I spent I would take it back in a heartbeat. No “promotion” Will ever get me sucked back in to such a poorly run company. This company is a nightmare.

Have had T-mobile for 11 days.  ALL incoming calls are going to voicemail.   I've been on the phone with customer support twice over 2 hours.  I was also supposed to receive Netflix and had to reach out for that promo, as it wasn't automatic.  Their customer service can't even return my call, because they too go to voicemail.  NEVER again and running back to Verizon!

T mobile customer service is terrible!! There is definitely a language barrier with their reps!! They don't understand English enough to fix the problem! Waste of time and money! I am completely turned off by companies who use these people to conduct their business here. Anything to save a penny and take away jobs from Americans.. Over it!

To say their customer service was bad would imply they actually have any.  Hands down T Mobile is the most greedy, worthless self centered corporate piece of garbage out there.  They clearly have decided its more profitable to offer no customer service than it is to offer any.  You can call but you get a low end call center half way across the world.  They lost me as a customer

Shady! I am a 20 year Sprint customer. After my Galaxy phone was outdated several years ago, I got a different model as suggested by a Sprint store associate....I was never able to locate a suitable protection case for this unpopular model, therefore, it became damaged very quickly....cracked screen etc....
I recently decided to upgrade, but researched for an Otter Box first. Okay.
I ordered the phone online...T-Mobile/Sprint took my money immediately, but never provided tracking info....it kept saying..."Order still processing"?
I received the phone via UPS unannounced today.
I began backing up my current phone to transfer all data. Suddenly, I had no call out service at all with either phone?
The new phone came with a Sprint sim card installed, but a T-Mobile sim card in the box. I used my home pc to go to Sprint.com to try to get atleast ONE PHONE WORKING AGAIN.
During activating the new phone, it was suggested that I install the T-Mobile sim card. When I did, a message appeared that my prices will change? But didn't say how much in either direction?
I thought this was totally shady, then I re-installed the already installed Sprint sim card to complete the activation.
But here's the kicker. I use my phone for work...traveling. T-Mobile/Sprint left me high and dry without service after normal business hours with no support!
By the time I got through their clusterf#ck activation process, I had already cancelled work!
A double kick in the nuts!
And I did as well on my own as any monkey who works for them could've done!
Unbelievable the lack of service received in such a money grubbing industry!!

I signed up for service on the website because the monthly fee was better than in the store. I spoke with customer care in sales and was told that the SIM card would be overnighted to me. I explained that I had to have my cell service working as soon as possible. Two full days went by and no SIM card so the next day I went to Xfinity and got service and a SIM card immediately. Two days later I called T-Mobile to cancel everything and they told me at customer service that I could not cancel my number/account until I returned the SIM card (which was free). I still have not gotten the card and finally one week later the It arrived so it took two weeks to get it. I called customer service and they told me that they could not cancel the phone number/account until they received the SIM card and they would send me a return mailing label.. Two more weeks went by and I still had not received the label. BUT I received a bill for a month of service. I called again and customer service said that they would send the label and then cancel the account/service. Next I get a bill from a debt collector saying that my credit is in jeopardy and I owe $115. There is no sense in calling customer service because they lie. I have decided to just pay the $115 and call it a learning experience. But at every opportunity I will give a negative review of my experiences with T-Mobile.

In January 2021 i had the most unpleasant customer service with T-Mobile. I previously had a contract with Sprint wireless, which was going well for me; but after the acquisition of Sprint by T-Mobile, i started to have a lot of problems with my bill that was set up for autopay. In January 2021 i noticed that they took out double the amount of $71.27 for the payment. After speaking with five different customer service representatives, including a supervisor, i was told that the reason for the additional amount was because i had made a long distance call to Canada. I then told the Supervisor that the plan that i had with Sprint allows me to call Canada and Mexico without incurring long distance charges: she insisted that it does not, and that if i wanted to continue to call Canada, i will have to start paying an additional $10. per month. After around 2 hours on the phone over 3 days, i decided that i would just wait until the end of my phone lease agreement in June, and just rid myself of this horrible and unethical company, T-Mobile!!!
Been with T-Mobile for years. Never had a issue!
I don’t even wanna a give one star horriable customer service how they put this kind of customer services never using t mobile accessories again

I’ve been dealing with this crappy T-MOBILE carrier for years only because I’m always busy at work or sleeping. This mobile carrier has the worst service I have ever experienced.. I could literally pull into the driveway leading to my residence and would hear my daughter telling me I can't hear anything but static...this was constantly poor service with drop calls and trying to search the internet on it was horrendous! After battling with it for months I called tech support and was told " it shouldn't do that." With absolutely no assistance to try and find out why or what to do to correct the problem. I finally couldn't handle it any more so I switched companies. However when I was switching companies I paid off everything I owed T Mobile or so they told me at that time. A month later they charged me another month of service. When I went into the store to find out why I was told it was the prior months service and I would still owe another week of service. I asked how that was possible when I paid a month in advance to start with. They just kept repeating the same thing. And my answer to them is if you pay in advance a month of service I should be credited not owing. Couldn't get anywhere with their customer service. I will never have T-Mobile service again and I will recommend to everyone I know to never get their service it was the worst I've ever experienced!

I am a long time customer of Sprint. I recently traded in my phone to sprint/t-mobile - there was no damage to my phone when I returned it. My Galaxy Note 10+ 5g was eligible to be unlocked, due to pay off on 05/11/2021. However, it is 06/16/2021 and my phone is still locked. On 05/26/2021 I was told I would receive 1 month of service for free, and on 06/11/2021 I was told I would receive $140.34 (previous months bill) credit. I only received a $50 credit on 06/08/2021. I have spoken with T-Mobile representatives no less than 23 times since 05/12/2021. I have been promised numerous calls back that I have never received. The representative I last spoke with on 06/11/2021 told me she did not have anyone else above her I could speak with as she is at the top of the chain. I was only asking that my phone be unlocked so I could switch providers as I was told I could do the day I purchased the phone. If the salesperson had explained everything honestly I would have never switched to T-Mobile in the first place.

I don't even want to give tmobile 1 star. They have maybe a handful of good and honest workers, but the rest are rude and inconsiderate at best. My husband and I have been loyal customers to them for THREE YEARS and we were paying our bill a year in advance for all 3 of those years. I went in one day because I was constantly having problems with calls always being dropped and never having good service, I talked to someone about using the money on our account to upgrade to a new phone and they said I COULDN'T do that and that they NEVER have anyone pay their account up to a year in advance so they couldn't use the money in the account that we paid in CASH to go to a new phone. Okay that's not a big deal, right? Well it gets better. We try to get our service fixed because we're we live (in a CITY) we canNOT get service. Calls drop all the time. The customer service rep informs me that they do not have sufficient towers where we are 🙃 wow, ok. So we decide to ask for a refund because we were literally standing in front of a t mobile store on the phone with a t mobile rep and could not get the call to stop dropping. We tell them we want to switch to a new phone company, everything seems fine, we get a full refund of everything we put on the account, then get told we have am outstanding bill for the last 3 months and we will be charged a late fee (because they refunded us ALL of the money ???) It gets better guys. We pay off our final bill, go to drop the phones that we had with tmobile off at the store, get told we have to ship them in, call customer service and they "create us a shipping label" and tole us it would be here in "about a week." A month rolls buy, no shipping label. We get a bill in the mail for 1500!!!!! We call, someone apologizes and says they are creating us a shipping label. THEY HANG UP ON US! When we call in again, we get told that we cannot return the phones because it's been past 20 days since we got them !!!! Even though we were told multiple times they were sending us a shipping label! The supervisor then tells us we were misinformed and that we HAVE to pay the 1500!!!!!! And get this, it's not just paying it in increments, they want the entire thing by the end of this month! Never bothered to call and tell us we were misinformed or anything!!! You surely do not want to be with this phone company! Three years of loyalty and they treat us like trash and have crap service! I would go back to snail mail before I would ever go back to t mobile!

I am so upset with my wife for switching to this terrible company I am gong to ask her for a divorce if I can ever get a call through to her. The texts don't go through either so that is out, the customer service is full of geek squad dropouts, and the new phone they talked her into buying is also just a piece of junk. I will tell everyone I know and shout it everywhere I go, "Stay away from this awful excuse for a company." Six years will a little company named Ting and not a problem one, Two months with T-mobile and not a day goes by without a problem.

if i could give a zero i would. i was a customer for 14 years and a few months after i left the company i start getting calls from a collections agency. turns out i owed $617 for unpaid bills because somehow, my account that has always been on autopay just got turn off. tmobile claims they don’t contact customers about late bills so i was never notified and assumed my bill was being kid monthly. and i learned it wasn’t through collections. really? y’all couldnt send a single letter or nothing after like the third month of nonpayment? turn of my phone to alert me or nothing? ridiculous!

Service is awful!! Everyday my phone doesn’t work for hours. Either the internet won’t work or it all doesn’t work. Switching ASAP!

I rarely give a negative review.  However, this is dreadful service!  Prior to the sprint/t-mobile merger, I had great Sprint service!  Now, I cannot make a call from my own home and even have limited service in my place of work!  I have called t mobile to trouble shoot, that was pointless.  Im so disappointed to be  paying premium prices for  terrible service.  I even purchased a brand new samsung S 21.   This failed to help resolve my connection issues as well.

There is no 24/7 customer service and you may be disconnected after spending up to 40 minutes on the phone waiting for help. 
Their offshore customer service is poorly trained and has actually made my service worse instead of improving it. 
I consider cell service an essential utility which may involve life and death situations. Since the merger (I was a Sprint customer), their failures, and then my inability to contact them for assistance, may have directly contributed to a serious medical situation. 
I'm sure that something I agreed to will prevent me from holding them responsible, but it can's stop me from warning you of their irresponsibility. 
I have been unable to contact them for over 18 hours, with them disconnecting my calls after extended hold times. 
Don't trust these folks for essential communications.

I went to the store in Charleston  West Virginia because my phone lost all signal and was just roaming these are the rudest people I have ever dealt with they didn't even acknowledge that I was in the store and then the next customer associate that came available just stood there and stared at me like I was in his way I said can you please help me and he said it depends on what it is with I told him my phone had no signal And I just paid my bill he said he couldn't help me because he didn't have any SIM cards matter of fact they wouldn't even let me upgrade  Because they had no SIM cards I said so I'm just out of luck He said basically because they were in the middle of buying out Sprint so there was nothing they could do for me T-Mobile is the worst cell phone Carrier I have ever dealt with if you're thinking about going with them a word to the wise is don't do it. If the devil himself started a wireless company and had Jack the Ripper as Director of First Impressions, I would use them over Sprint/T Mobile any day of the week and twice on Sunday! They didn't order my phone, had to get one from Best Buy...only worked with Sprint, not T Mobile....so I had to send phone back to Sprint and order another one through T Mobile...I got Sprint bills for 6 months and couldn't pay them because no one at their call center in Pakistan could find my account (because I never had one!)...Took 2 months to port over my number from AT&T....while getting 3 different cell phone bills....finally got someone to locate the account before they sent the $27 bill to collections while I'm in the proces of buying a house and almost ruined it...WORST COMPANY I HAVE EVER DEALT WITH and I'm almost 50....I haven't even mentioned that their wireless service sucks and is slow as h$ll....I'd rather smear honey all over my body and lie on an ant hill than to deal with these people ever again!!!! If this review changes your mind about switching to T Mobile -- YOU'RE WELCOME!! I should start a Go Fund ME account for helping people avoid disaster...and it can pay off that $27 I was forced to pay for a phone and service I never had with a company that kind of exists, but not really.....WTH!!!???

T-Mobile is just terrible and dishonest.
Two months ago, I suddenly lost access to the network. The next day, I went to the store about it and was told it was my phone. I was not convinced but they left me no other choice than buying a new handset.
It turned out that I had lost access because it was in fact T-Mobile who had improperly assigned my line number to a SIM card on another phone, something known in the industry as a SIM swap.
Then the store had in fact concealed the real cause and deceived me into buying a new handset, even charging me an upgrade fee.
When I complained, T-Mobile nickel & dimed me.
I filed a complaint with both the Better Business Bureau and the Federal Communications Commission and T-Mobile responded with a bunch of lies, trying to sweep the issues under the rug and avoid any regulatory problems. 
I suspect that thousands of other customers have been deceived the same way.

Horrible customer service. Had a problem with my phone called them 2 times. The last time i called the was on hold for almost two hours. When i did get in contact with tech support, they had no idea what they were doing. My calls kept getting dropped from a phone i bought from them, that they promoted at the T mobile store in North Port Florida. I had to fight with the to replace the phone with something better, and the said they would send me out  a iphone se 2020 with a case for free. I was happy with that . When i asked what to do with the other phone that i had they said keep it ! And told me I'm a valued customer and would send me the iPhone with a choice of any phone case i wanted. But turned out nothing was free they were going to charge me the monthly price for the phone. T mobile needs to get there act together i might change my phone service. Been with them over 10 years. I would not recommend going with T mobile !

On June 6th, 2021 my Iphone dropped into the lake and could not be retrieved so I was already upset. I was looking up different options to buy a refurbished Iphone and then decided to contact T-Mobile to see what they can do to help me in this situation. I didn’t have much faith but I wanted to give it a try because what is the worst thing that could happen? Well let me tell you…

 

I contacted a T-Mobile expert via chat on the website by the name of Nina. She was helpful and was set on helping me solve the problem. She asked if there was another number I could use to talk to her so she can offer me a solution. I used my boyfriend’s phone to chat with her and was offered T-Mobile’s protection 360 plan. She said since I’ve been a loyal customer, she will offer this one time and I can file a claim saying I lost my phone once the plan is in my account. She said they will send me a replacement of the same model of the phone I had lost. All I had to do was pay a small deductible of $250. I was more than glad and she even mentioned that she will waive this month’s billing for the plan which is $9 so the first bill will be July. She then provided me with steps to file a claim as well and that was when our conversation ended. 

 

Once the protection plan showed up on my account, I immediately noticed that the price was different. This protection plan was pending for $18 this month when I was promised a fee waiver and the price given to me was actually $9. I contacted another expert via T-Mobile website and they were able to credit me the $18 for this month and said that starting next month the fee is $9. Perfect.

 

Also, since the protection was visible on my account, I went ahead and filed a claim on mytmoclaim.com and got denied. I contacted them and was told, my phone was not covered because I lost it before I signed up for the protection plan. Understandable but I was promised by a T-Mobile expert that I would be covered this one time. Confused, I contacted another expert on the website and was told that I cannot be covered. Voneymard, the expert I spoke with said they can only offer me the $250 in credit (equivalent to the deductible fee that I would have paid for the replacement under the protection 360 if I upgrade my phone and buy a brand new one).

 

This whole experience was completely unacceptable and seemed like a total fraud. I would not be upset if they simple told me there was nothing they could do because I did not have their protection 360 plan. They promised me all of this if I signed up for the plan and even charged me more than promised. It is now June 9th, 2021 and I am at square one again because T-Mobile thinks it’s okay for their “experts” to promise customers something that is not even TRUE. I would simply accept the truth and not be angry because it is my fault for not purchasing the protection 360 plan in the first place. Now I am “offered” the $250 in credit to upgrade to a brand new Iphone 12 Pro. Definitely a scam to push their customers to spend more money. I DO NOT recommend a company like this. Promise your loyal customers if there is a possible solution. If you think you can make money this way then your whole company is a SCAM.

I came into Tmoblie to see why my phone didn't come with a charger box they explained to me and advised how I could go on the website to see why it didn't come with phone but Ms Gina and Mr Jaron helped me with getting a my charger box and also suggested a new screen protector and phone case and they were very polite and helpful thank u guys so much

They have no knowledge of how to correct anything. Customer service are robotic when speaking. Frustrating,irritating and very very stressful.This  is the worse service. I can’t make calls texts or download. They’ve sent me SIM cards and talked me into upgrades and charging more than contract and the service does not work. Please run and stay safe from this horrible service

Just a word of acknowledgement for a great T-Mobil representative named Martina.. She is extremely knowledgable, friendly, and patient.  She should be commended for her attention to detail and her compassion towards her customers needs. She is the epitome of the perfect customer service personality. 
She was able to help me with problems concerning my phone and knowledgeable to tell me helpful tips for the future. I highly recommend Martina from 32805 Eland Blvd. In Zephyrhills.  Thanks again Martina.. From Louise in WeekiWachee .

I have been with TMOBILE for about 15 years. It use to be a great company,  but not so much now.  TMOBILE  company misrepresent their plans and I truly believe they rig their network site to use up your hotspot faster, so they can slow your streaming down. They say you have unlimited hotspot and data, but you really only have 50 gigs of hotspot and 30 gigs of data before they drastically slow you down to the point that it becomes useless.  TMOBILE advertise that they will only slow you down when there is congestion after using 50 gigs of hotspot.  Well, congestion must be 24/7.  Also, TMOBILE says that they will only slow you down to the point that it may take 3 minutes to load a movie rather than 30 seconds.  Well I found that to be untruthful.  Also, TMOBILE does not honor the deals. This company will try everything to get you to chose a higher rate plan. Expecailly since they merged with Sprint.  Their coverage, service and customer services has drastically declined. TMOBILE use to be an A+ company when I signed up with them 15 years ago, but now they are a C- company that is only out to make money rather than providing quality, fair and honest service to their customers!!!

Recently transferred 4 phone lines and 1 IPad line.  Worst mistake I have ever made with cell phone service.  Terrible coverage, usually 1 or 2 bars.  Dropped calls almost daily.  I will try to leave T-Mobile ASAP.

I am a 24 year sprint customer. After requesting to speak to my Loyalty Team many times, I was told they didn't have one. However, I was asked why do I need to speak with them??? I said I wanted to speak with them or I was leaving. I then started an email chat. I have always did a 2 yr contract for a free phone x (4). I finally got most of my issue resolved, but then was turned over to a sales rep. He was very forceful about me getting the insurance that I did not want. I always have old phones for back up. He then told me my old phones would no longer work after the first of the year (Lie). I ended the call saying I would leave. Then within minutes, I got emails telling me about a sims card that would allow my iphone 8 to work. I have put the sims card in my phone and I stay at 1 bar consistently.  After being lied to and poor reception Looks like I will be leaving.....SAD I miss sprint and my Loyalty Team!!!!!
